title: Neottopteris nidus
taxonomy: Neottopteris nidus is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Neottopteris, which is a group of ferns found in tropical regions. Neottopteris nidus is a type of epiphytic fern, meaning it grows on other plants for support. The species is native to Southeast Asia, where it can be found growing on trees and shrubs. Neottopteris nidus is also known as the "tropical roof fern" due to its unique habit of growing on top of other plants.
uses: Neottopteris nidus, also known as the treetop fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and relieve pain. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing. Additionally, Neottopteris nidus is thought to have antioxidant effects, which could help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

intro: Neottopteris nidus, commonly known as the nest fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The health benefits of Neottopteris nidus include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Neottopteris nidus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neottopteris nidus is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, often combined with other herbs, to create remedies for respiratory issues, fever, and skin problems.
